title: Team Teaching: The Giant Classroom Experiment
subtitle: 一间教室四位老师：团队教学正在改变美国高中

paragraphs.en: One hundred thirty-five students, four teachers, one giant classroom: This is what 9th grade looks like at Westwood High School, in Mesa, Arizona's largest school system. There, an innovative teaching model has taken hold, and is spreading to other schools in the district and beyond. Five years ago, faced with high teacher turnover and declining student enrollment, Westwood's leaders decided to try something different.
paragraphs.zh: 135名学生，4位老师，一间巨型教室：这就是亚利桑那州最大公立学区梅萨市韦斯特伍德高中九年级的模样。在那里，一种创新的教学模式已经扎根，并正推广到学区内外的其他学校。五年前，面对教师高流动率和学生入学人数下降的局面，韦斯特伍德的校领导决定尝试些不一样的举措。

paragraphs.en: Working with professors at Arizona State University's teachers college, they piloted a classroom model known as team teaching, which allows teachers to dissolve the walls that separate their classes across physical or grade divides. The teachers share large groups of students and rotate between group instruction, one-on-one interventions, or whatever the teachers agree is a priority that day.
paragraphs.zh: 他们与亚利桑那州立大学师范学院的教授合作，试点了一种名为“团队教学”的课堂模式。该模式允许教师打破物理空间或年级界限的隔阂。教师们共同负责一大群学生，并在大组教学、一对一辅导、小组学习或团队当天商定的任何优先事项之间灵活轮换。

paragraphs.en: What looks at times like chaos is in fact a carefully orchestrated plan: Each morning, the Westwood teams meet to hammer out a personalized program for every student the team will focus on that day. By giving teachers more opportunity to collaborate, Mesa's administrators hoped to fill staffing gaps and boost teacher morale and retention.
paragraphs.zh: 看似混乱的场景，实则是精心编排的计划：每天早晨，韦斯特伍德的教师团队都会碰头，为当天要重点关注的学生逐一敲定个性化方案。梅萨学区的管理者希望通过给予教师更多协作机会，来填补人员缺口，提振教师士气并留住人才。

paragraphs.en: Initial research suggests the gamble could pay off. "Teachers are doing fantastic things, but it's very rare a teacher walks into another room to see what's happening," said Andi Fourlis, superintendent of Mesa Public Schools. "Our profession is so slow to advance because we are working in isolation."
paragraphs.zh: 初步研究表明，这场豪赌可能获得回报。梅萨公立学校学区总监安迪·福里斯说：“老师们都在做着了不起的事，但很少有老师会走进另一间教室看看发生了什么。我们这个职业之所以进步如此缓慢，正是因为我们总是在孤立地工作。”

paragraphs.en: Of course, overhauling teaching approaches can't fix all the frustrations teachers have, but early results from Mesa show team teaching may be helping to reverse low morale. In a survey, researchers found those who worked on teams reported greater job satisfaction, more frequent collaborations with colleagues, and more positive interactions with students.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，彻底改革教学方法并不能解决教师面临的所有困扰，但梅萨学区的早期结果表明，团队教学可能有助于扭转低落的士气。在一项调查中，研究人员发现，参与团队教学的教师工作满意度更高，与同事的合作更频繁，与学生的互动也更积极。

paragraphs.en: Another benefit of teams, teachers say, is that they can help each other improve their instruction. During one planning session, English teacher Jeff Hall shared a performance appraisal with a science teacher: Her recent lecture on "the central dogma of biology" had bewildered him and their other teammates. "If the science is too confusing for me, can you imagine the frustration you feel as kids?" Hall said.
paragraphs.zh: 老师们表示，团队教学的另一个好处是能互相帮助改进教学。在一次备课会上，英语老师杰夫·霍尔向一位科学老师分享了一条绩效反馈：她最近关于“生物学中心法则”的讲座让他和团队其他成员听得一头雾水。霍尔说：“如果科学内容连我都觉得太难懂，你能想象孩子们会有多沮丧吗？”

paragraphs.en: But the science teacher wouldn't have known about the confusion on her own. The model is not for everyone. Some teachers approached about volunteering for a team have said they prefer to work alone. Team teaching can also be a scheduling nightmare, especially where only some staff work in teams.
paragraphs.zh: 但若仅凭她自己，那位科学老师可能根本意识不到学生的困惑。这种模式并非适合所有人。一些被征询是否愿意加入团队的教师表示，他们更愿意独自工作。团队教学也可能给排课带来巨大挑战，尤其在只有部分教师参与团队教学的学校里。

paragraphs.en: There are also thorny questions like how to evaluate four teachers on the performance of 135 students. But for the time being, it seems to be working.
paragraphs.zh: 此外还有一些棘手的问题，例如如何根据135名学生的表现来评估4位老师。但就目前而言，这种模式似乎正在发挥作用。
